name: Old-Fashioned Spice Cake1
recipeIngredient: If using self-rising flour, omit vinegar, baking soda, and salt.
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a rectangular pan, 13 x 9 x 2 inches, or two round pans, 8 x 1-1/2 inches, or 9 x 1-1/2 inches.
2. Mix 1-1/3 cups evaporated milk with vinegar in a bowl; reserve the remaining mixture for frosting.
3. In another bowl, combine all ingredients except caramel frosting on low speed for 30 seconds, scraping the bowl constantly. Beat on high speed for 3 minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally.
4. Pour batter into prepared pan(s).
5. Bake rectangle for about 45 minutes, or round pans (8-inch rounds) for 40-45 minutes, or (9-inch rounds) for 35-40 minutes, until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
6. Cool cake on a wire rack. Cool layers for 10 minutes, then remove from pans and cool completely on a wire rack.
7. Frost rectangle with caramel frosting or fill and frost layered cake. Sprinkle with chopped nuts or ground nutmeg if desired. Store tightly covered.
16 servings; approximately 435 calories per serving.
